title: Etsy stock pops 14% on sale of Depop to eBay for $1.2 billion
text: Etsy announced the sale of Depop to eBay for $1.2 billion in cash, prompting Etsy shares to jump 14% on the news. The sale represents a strategic shift for Etsy to focus on its core marketplace. Etsy acquired Depop for $1.625 billion in 2021, representing a significant loss of approximately $425 million. The transaction is expected to close by the end of Q3 2026. Depop had achieved strong growth, with 2024 full-year GMS climbing 32% to $788.9 million and U.S. Depop sales surging approximately 60%, making it the fastest-growing fashion resale platform domestically. However, the lower adjusted EBITDA margins were a 350 basis-point drag to Etsy's overall profitability. The divestiture will allow Etsy to focus on improving returns on capital in its core marketplace while pursuing AI-driven growth initiatives. Etsy's CFO Lanny Baker stated the company wants to focus on areas where they can achieve higher returns on capital.
